ical appliances, educational and artisticprogress,

pants in that memorable and sanguinary contest , but to all sol-

of the United Confederate Veterans has felt constrained to ac-
cept the invitation, in the spirit in which it was given , and with
the hope and belief that it may promote the patriotic purpose of
re-uniting the sections of this great country in the bonds of
perpetual friendship and unity , and ,
Whereas , this Legislature , fresh from the people , desires to
place themselves on record as the representatives of brave and
chivalrous Kentuckians , that we heartily commend the spirit
in which this invitation to has been extended and regarded it a
duty to respond as best we may to this genuine overture of
our former foes, destined , we trust, under the providence of

CHAPTER 103 .
AN ACT to enlarge the usefulness of the Kentucky Normal and
Industrial Institute for Colored Persons .
Whereas , The Kentucky Normal and Industrial Institute for
colored persons is doing a highly creditable and important
work developing good and useful citizens, and
Whereas , That school is hindered in its good work because of
indebtedness , needed buildings and equipment as pointed out in
the report of State Inspector and Examiner , and
Whereas , This Commonwealth has never made an appropri-
ation for the maintenance of industrial or trade training for its
negro citizens , therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth ofKentucky :
§1 . That the sum of seventeen thousand and five
hundred dollars($17,500.00) be appropriatedtoThe
Kentucky Normal and IndustrialInstitutefor Col-
ored Persons from any funds in the State Treasury
not otherwiseappropriatedfor the purpose of set-
tling an indebtedness of five thousand dollars
($5,000.00);a centralheatingplant,eightthousand
dollars($8,000.00); a laundry, twenty-fivehundred
dollars($2,500.00).
§2 . That the sum of two thousand dollars ($2,-
000.00) annuallybe appropriatedfor the equipment
and maintenance of the training of students in such
useful trades as the Board of Trustees in its wisdom
may direct.
Approved by the Governor , March 15 , 1912 .

ernor .
